text: Manage course participation by understanding grading policies, following the schedule, using communication channels, and correctly submitting exercises.
Obtain, configure, and monitor free‑tier cloud accounts (AWS, GCP, Azure) to prevent unexpected costs.
Use tunneling services (e.g., ngrok, localtunnel, serveo) to expose locally running applications to the internet.

Analyze the advantages, cost model, and potential disadvantages of cloud computing, such as economic benefits, elasticity, rapid deployment, reduced capital investment, pay-per-use pricing, trust issues, vendor lock-in, and interoperability challenges.
Implement a lightweight Go web application with HTMX that provides CRUD operations and dynamic views for books, authors, and years.
Deploy the application to cloud environments, understanding cloud development requirements, deployment modes, transparent infrastructure management, and account login considerations.
Analyze memory hierarchy challenges and apply shared- and distributed-memory programming models, including NUMA and MPI.
Compare high-performance networking technologies and architectures, including Ethernet, VLANs, InfiniBand, and Software-Defined Networking.
Compare virtualization and containerization, describing multi‑tenancy, resource isolation, scalability, and cost differences.
Build, push, and orchestrate containerized applications using Docker commands and Docker Compose, including multi‑container setups and submission of artifacts.

Use OS‑level container technologies and Docker components—including namespaces, cgroups, images, union filesystems, registries, Compose, and Swarm—to deploy and manage isolated applications, and compare containers with virtual machines.
Operate VMware vSphere to provision, configure, monitor, and migrate virtual machines and resources, utilizing vCenter, storage networks, vMotion, DRS, and related management tasks.
Compare monolithic and microservice architectures, including their characteristics, advantages, disadvantages, and trade‑offs.
Compare AWS storage services (EBS, instance store, EFS, S3), their characteristics, and cost considerations.
Apply infrastructure-as-code tools (CloudFormation, Terraform) and understand multi‑cloud management, pricing, and cost‑optimization strategies.
Collect, store, and visualize metrics using common monitoring tools such as Amazon CloudWatch, Prometheus, and Grafana, and create custom metrics.
Manage and analyze log data with formats, the ELK stack, and CloudWatch Logs, applying common use cases like root‑cause analysis.
Instrument, collect, and interpret distributed tracing data, understanding spans, context propagation, sampling, overhead mitigation, and open‑source tracing tools.
Compare vertical and horizontal scaling, including their advantages, disadvantages, and the architectural requirements for horizontal scaling.
Apply AWS autoscaling features by identifying its core components and configuring target‑tracking, simple, step, and predictive scaling policies.

Deploy and manage application workloads in Kubernetes using Pods, Deployments, StatefulSets, Services, ConfigMaps, and Persistent Volumes, and understand their declarative configuration and lifecycle.

Apply Kubernetes autoscaling mechanisms—including Horizontal Pod Autoscaler, Vertical Pod Autoscaler, and Cluster Autoscaler—by selecting appropriate metrics and policies to adjust pod and node resources.
Implement monitoring and observability in Kubernetes with Prometheus (operator and ServiceMonitor) and recognize alternative monitoring solutions.
Compare major managed Kubernetes services, their features, billing models, and explain why Kubernetes serves as a cloud‑native platform for applications.
Compare lightweight sandbox technologies for FaaS such as gVisor, Firecracker, Kata Containers, and WebAssembly, and evaluate their isolation and performance trade-offs.
Configure and select container runtimes in Kubernetes using RuntimeClasses and related tools like Knative.
Write a simple Java handler for a FaaS function using a lightweight framework.
Explain the AWS Shared Responsibility Model and select appropriate AWS services to meet compliance and regulatory requirements.

Design and manage identity and access controls in AWS, including IAM roles, policies, federation, least‑privilege permissions, secrets handling, and authentication standards such as OAuth and Cognito.
Implement multi‑account governance using AWS Organizations, Control Tower, and guardrails to isolate workloads and enforce security policies across accounts.
Apply encryption and key management to protect data in transit and at rest, using TLS, ACM, Private CA, KMS, and service‑specific encryption options.
Architect and secure AWS network infrastructure, including VPC design, subnets, routing, NACLs, security groups, firewalls, WAF/Shield, and DDoS mitigation techniques.
Establish automated, continuous security operations with logging, monitoring, patch and configuration management, and managed security services.
Apply Terraform for Infrastructure‑as‑Code by interpreting configurations, provisioning AWS resources, and following the plan‑review‑apply workflow.
Use Policy‑as‑Code with OPA and Regula to define, evaluate, and enforce compliance policies on IaC files.
Outline the end‑to‑end cloud‑native application deployment automation pipeline, linking build, infrastructure provisioning, policy checks, and GitOps‑driven continuous delivery.

Describe and configure core AWS serverless services (Lambda, API Gateway, S3 static hosting, Cognito, DynamoDB, IAM), including pricing, permissions, and building a simple end‑to‑end serverless application.
Utilize the Serverless Framework to define, deploy, and monitor serverless functions across multiple cloud providers.
Compare the three primary cloud storage service models (object, block, and file), their key characteristics, typical use cases, and the corresponding AWS offerings (S3, EBS, EFS).
Compare and evaluate IoT communication technologies and standards (e.g., Bluetooth, ZigBee, Wi‑Fi, LoRaWAN, Thread, Matter) and related ecosystem services for interoperability and performance.
Apply IoT design principles to develop and prototype a solution, including network topology design, simulation, digital twin modeling, and a monitoring application.
Design and implement infrastructure as code and automated CI/CD pipelines for cloud deployments.
Apply governance, security, and cost-management practices using policies, roles, tagging, and resource identifiers.
Implement observability, monitoring, and debugging techniques to ensure reliability of cloud-native systems.
Adopt cloud-native operational practices including environment segregation, DevOps integration, and managed service utilization.
